A primitive root; propr. To be complete, but used only as a denominative from sheba'; to seven oneself, i.e. Swear.
Alive, raw, fresh, strong, life. From chayah; alive; hence, raw; fresh, strong; also life, whether literally or figuratively.
Half. From chatsah; the half or middle.
Apartness, sacredness. From qadash; a sacred place or thing; rarely abstract, sanctity.
From amam; a people; specifically, a tribe; hence troops or attendants; figuratively, a flock.
To be complete, at an end, finished, accomplished, or spent. A primitive root; to end, whether intransitive or transitived.
To dash to pieces, scatter. A primitive root; to dash to pieces, or scatter.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
These. Prolonged from 'el; these or those.
To be complete, at an end, finished, accomplished, or spent. A primitive root; to end, whether intransitive or transitived.
To hear. A primitive root; to hear intelligently.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Lord. Or adon; from an unused root; sovereign, i.e. Controller.
The after-part, end. From 'achar; the last or end, hence, the future; also posterity.
These. Prolonged from 'el; these or those.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
God is my judge, an Isr. leader in Bab. (Aramaic) corresponding to Daniye'l; Danijel, the Heb. Prophet.
To utter, say. A primitive root; to say.
Speech, word. From dabar; a word; by implication, a matter or thing; adverbially, a cause.
To stop up, shut up, keep close. Or satham; a primitive root; to stop up; by implication, to repair; figuratively, to keep secret.
To seal, affix a seal, seal up. A primitive root; to close up; especially to seal.
As far as, even to, up to, until, while. Properly, the same as ad; as far as, whether of space or time or degree.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
End. Contracted from qatsats; an extremity; adverbially after.
Much, many, great. By contracted from rabab; abundant.
To purify, select. A primitive root; to clarify, examine, select.
To be, white, to make bricks. A primitive root; to be white; also to make bricks.
To smelt, refine, test. A primitive root; to fuse, i.e. Refine.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To be wicked, act wickedly. A primitive root; to be wrong; by implication, to disturb, violate.
Not. Or lowi; or loh; a primitive particle; not; by implication, no; often used with other particles.
The whole, all. Or kowl; from kalal; properly, the whole; hence, all, any or every.
Wicked, criminal. From rasha'; morally wrong; concretely, an bad person.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
To be, circumspect, intelligent. A primitive root; to be circumspect and hence, intelligent.
To discern. A primitive root; to separate mentally, i.e.(generally) understand.
Time. From ad; time, especially now, when, etc.
From an unused root meaning to stretch; properly, continuance; but used only constant; ellipt. The regular sacrifice.
To turn aside. Or suwr; a primitive root; to turn off.
Detested thing. Or shiqquts; from shaqats; disgusting, i.e. Filthy; especially idolatrous or an idol.
To be desolated or appalled. A primitive root; to stun, i.e. Devastate or stupefy.
To give, put, set. A primitive root; to give, used with greatest latitude of application.
A thousand. Prop, the same as 'eleph; hence a thousand.
Hundred. Or metyah; properly, a primitive numeral; a hundred; also as a multiplicative and a fraction.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
Happiness, blessedness. From 'ashar; happiness; only in masculine plural construction as interjection, how happy!
To wait, await. A primitive root; properly, to adhere to; hence, to await.
A primitive root; properly, to touch, i.e. Lay the hand upon; by implication, to reach; violently, to strike.
A thousand. Prop, the same as 'eleph; hence a thousand.
A three, triad. Or shalosh; masculine shlowshah; or shloshah; a primitive number; three; occasionally third, or Thrice.
Hundred. Or metyah; properly, a primitive numeral; a hundred; also as a multiplicative and a fraction.
Thirty. Or shloshiym; multiple of shalowsh; thirty; or thirtieth.
Five. Masculine chamishshah; a primitive numeral; five.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
To go, come, walk. Akin to yalak; a primitive root; to walk.
End. Contracted from qatsats; an extremity; adverbially after.
To take one's stand, stand. A primitive root; to stand, in various relations.
Or goral; from an unused root meaning to be rough; properly, a pebble, i.e. A lot; figuratively, a portion or destiny.
End. Contracted from qatsats; an extremity; adverbially after.
Day. From an unused root meaning to be hot; a day, whether literal, or figurative.
